objective: Find safe speedups for parseAspDocument, buildVirtualDocuments, htmlVirtualDocument, cssVirtualDocument, javascriptVirtualDocument.
focus: duplicate parsing, virtual document generation, source-map allocation, benchmark/product path mismatch
result_json: {"packet_id":"P1","findings":[{"title":"Reuse the Rust nested-region scan when building virtual document text and source maps","files":["crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:4962","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5069","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5118","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5162"],"evidence":"Rust build_virtual_documents sorts all regions once, but then filters/clones per language (4976-4981). For each non-HTML owner region, masked_region_content scans all_regions (5170-5174) and source_map_segments_for_region scans all_regions again (5125-5130). The TypeScript path already avoids a full scan by lower-bounding into sortedRegions for each owner in packages/core/src/virtual-documents.ts:175-188.","proposed_change":"Keep regions sorted, add a Rust helper equivalent to nestedRegionsForOwner that returns the owner-contained slice/Vec once, then pass that nested list to both masked_region_content and source_map_segments_for_region. Also bucket language regions without cloning EmbeddedRegion where practical. This preserves region ordering, source-map segment fields, and public API shape.","risk":"Low to medium. The main risk is accidentally changing nested hole inclusion/exclusion, especially owner self-exclusion and ASP-hole filtering. Golden tests should cover nested <% %> inside style/script plus style-attribute CSS wrappers.","expected_perf_effect":"Cuts repeated O(owner_regions * all_regions) scans during cssVirtualDocument/javascriptVirtualDocument/jscript generation; likely the safest large win for huge/generated files with many embedded ASP holes."},{"title":"Avoid repeated UTF-16-to-byte rescans in Rust virtual document construction","files":["crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5027","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5069","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5242","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5290","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5296","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:5307"],"evidence":"slice_utf16 converts both start and end offsets by scanning from the beginning of source_text every call (5290-5293, 5311-5327). mask_utf16_range calls slice_utf16 before walking chars (5296-5304). previous_significant_char slices the whole preceding owner span before reversing chars (5242-5246). build_concatenated_virtual_document also calls utf16_len(&text) on the growing virtual text for every region (5093-5097), which rescans accumulated output.","proposed_change":"Introduce a per-document UTF-16 index in the Rust builder (or a small local helper struct) that maps UTF-16 offsets to byte offsets once, then slice/mask by byte ranges. Track virtual UTF-16 length incrementally using prefix/content/suffix lengths instead of utf16_len(&text) on the growing string. Rework previous_significant_char to walk the indexed byte range backward without allocating a slice String.","risk":"Medium. Must preserve UTF-16 offsets and reject offsets that split surrogate pairs exactly as utf16_to_byte_offset does today. Needs tests with non-BMP characters, CRLF, and ASP holes in CSS/JS.","expected_perf_effect":"Reduces virtual document construction from many whole-document scans and temporary String allocations to mostly linear work; should improve htmlVirtualDocument masking and css/javascript virtual generation on large documents."},{"title":"Add a lazy single-language virtual document path for position and CSS-only features","files":["crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:1776","crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:1786","crates/asp-lsp-server/src/main.rs:531","crates/asp-lsp-server/src/main.rs:589","crates/asp-lsp-server/src/main.rs:665"],"evidence":"embedded_virtual_document_at currently calls embedded_virtual_documents and builds every mapped virtual document before finding the one containing the source position (1776-1799). embedded_position_feature then calls open_virtual_documents, which calls embedded_virtual_documents again to collect all virtual texts (main.rs:531-551, 665-671). Color presentations call embedded_virtual_documents even though only css is used (main.rs:589-605). Salsa may cache the second all-doc call, but the first request still constructs unrelated languages.","proposed_change":"Add internal Rust APIs such as embedded_virtual_document_at_with_open_virtuals and/or embedded_virtual_document_for_language that reuse the same cached all-doc result when open_virtuals are required, and build only CSS for CSS-only features when open_virtuals are not semantically needed. Keep existing public APIs as wrappers to preserve callers.","risk":"Medium. Sidecar operations may rely on open_virtuals for cross-language context, so do not drop open_virtuals for diagnostics/document-wide features without validating sidecar behavior. CSS colorPresentation is the lowest-risk targeted path because it only sends a CSS virtual range to the active CSS document.","expected_perf_effect":"Avoids constructing/remapping unrelated HTML/JS/CSS documents on single-position and CSS-only requests; improves latency for hover/completion/documentColor/colorPresentation on files with many embedded regions."},{"title":"Make the benchmark measure virtual-document generation separately from duplicate parsing","files":["scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs:41","scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs:47","scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs:105","scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs:170"],"evidence":"benchmark-huge measures parseAspDocument separately (41-44), but its buildVirtualDocuments benchmark reparses every source before building virtual docs (47-50). embedded-language-benchmark also parses inside every operation (105-108), while buildEmbeddedVirtualDocument then benchmarks only a single requested language through core.buildVirtualDocument (170-190). Production Rust uses parse_asp once through document_summary/virtual_documents (crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs:169-190), so the current benchmark names can hide whether time is parse, virtual generation, or sidecar work.","proposed_change":"Add benchmark variants that preparse once per run/source and then measure buildVirtualDocuments/buildVirtualDocument alone, while keeping the current end-to-end variants under explicit names such as parsePlusBuildVirtualDocuments. For product-path confidence, add a Rust/stdio benchmark or server request benchmark for embedded_virtual_documents instead of relying only on packages/core/dist.","risk":"Low. Benchmark-only change, no product behavior. Risk is comparability with older numbers; mitigate by printing operation names that say whether parsing is included.","expected_perf_effect":"No direct runtime speedup, but prevents optimizing the wrong layer and makes duplicate parsing visible. It should make safe virtual-doc changes easier to validate against product behavior."},{"title":"Reduce TypeScript SourceMap allocation when segments are already source-sorted","files":["packages/core/src/virtual-documents.ts:91","packages/core/src/virtual-documents.ts:120","packages/core/src/virtual-documents.ts:328","packages/core/src/virtual-documents.ts:359"],"evidence":"createSourceMap always allocates and sorts sourceSegments with [...segments].sort (359-366). The callers build segments in source order for masked HTML (328-356) and for concatenated docs when regions are in source order and sourceMapSegmentsForRegion walks holes in order (91-112, 120-138). SourceMap.segments is already used for virtual-offset binary search, so this extra copy only helps if source order differs from virtual order.","proposed_change":"Keep SourceMap.segments unchanged, but only allocate/sort a separate sourceSegments array when a cheap monotonic check shows segments are not source-sorted; otherwise reuse segments for toVirtualOffset. If not all callers can guarantee ordering, perform the monotonic check inside createSourceMap to preserve the public API.","risk":"Low. The only behavior risk is binary-searching an unsorted array; the monotonic guard avoids that. Add tests for toVirtualOffset/toSourceOffset with HTML, CSS style attributes, and multiple script/style regions.","expected_perf_effect":"Small to moderate reduction in allocation/sort cost for htmlVirtualDocument/cssVirtualDocument/javascriptVirtualDocument, especially on documents with many source-map segments."}],"verification":["Read-only static inspection only; no files edited, staged, committed, or pushed.","Inspected the requested focus sources: packages/core/src/parser.ts, packages/core/src/virtual-documents.ts (requested path appears as virtual-documents.ts in this checkout), crates/asp-ide/src/lib.rs, scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs, and scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs.","Did not run benchmark-huge.mjs or embedded-language-benchmark.mjs because their generators may write sample outputs, which would violate the read-only packet constraint."],"blockers":[]}

objective: Find safe speedups for collectVbscriptSymbols and analyzeVbscript.
focus: duplicate parsing, JSON cloning, cache key misses, repeated symbols/diagnostics work
result_json: {"packet_id":"P2","findings":[{"title":"Check semantic caches before constructing VbAnalysisCache","files":["crates/asp-analysis/src/core_bridge.rs"],"evidence":"The bridge constructs VbAnalysisCache before calling cached_collect_symbols_with_analysis for collectVbscriptSymbols (lines 155-163) and before checking symbol/diagnostic caches for analyzeVbscript (lines 203-214). VbAnalysisCache::new then slices every VB region, tokenizes it, filters significant tokens, and builds a VB CST (lines 2749-2785). Cache hits only happen later inside cached_collect_symbols_with_analysis/cached_diagnostics_with_analysis (lines 273-284 and 296-307), after that parse/tokenize cost has already been paid.","proposed_change":"For requests with a usable semantic cache key, probe symbol_cache before VbAnalysisCache::new in collectVbscriptSymbols/collectVbscriptSymbolsFromText. In analyzeVbscript/analyzeVbscriptFromText, if both symbols and diagnostics are cached, return them before building VbAnalysisCache; if only symbols are cached, build VbAnalysisCache only for the missing diagnostics path. Keep the existing cache value shape and clone-on-return behavior.","risk":"Low. The change only reorders cache lookup ahead of pure analysis construction. Care is needed to preserve current cache invalidation/clear behavior and to avoid using diagnostics cached under a mismatched context key.","expected_perf_effect":"Large on hot semantic-cache paths because it avoids full VB region tokenization and CST construction. No effect on cold-cache paths."},{"title":"Share parsed analysis skeletons across FromText VB operations","files":["crates/asp-analysis/src/core_bridge.rs","packages/core/src/vbscript.ts","scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs"],"evidence":"Rust FromText operations create a fresh analysis skeleton directly: collectVbscriptSymbolsFromText uses parse_asp_document_analysis_skeleton (lines 165-180), analyzeVbscriptFromText does the same (lines 216-234), and summarize also does so (lines 189-201). The helper itself parses the ASP skeleton and inserts text (lines 646-651). In TS, collectVbscriptSymbolsFromTextAsync and analyzeVbscriptFromTextAsync have operation-specific result caches and each cold path calls parseAspDocument (vbscript.ts lines 1799-1816 and 3044-3063); the cache key includes operation (lines 3185-3205). benchmark-huge runs collectVbscriptSymbols and analyzeVbscript as separate operations over the same source set (lines 54-63).","proposed_change":"Add an op-agnostic parsed-analysis-skeleton cache keyed by the document cacheKey or by the same uri/text/settings identity used for FromText cache validation, then use it from collect/analyze/summary FromText paths. On the TS fallback path, consider a small shared parsed-document cache for FromText cold paths so collect followed by analyze does not parse the same text twice. Keep final result caches operation-specific.","risk":"Low to medium. Safe if the cache key includes text/settings and if cached parsed objects are not mutated after parsing. Verify skeleton hydration and UTF-16 ranges remain unchanged.","expected_perf_effect":"Medium to large when multiple VB operations run on the same document in one benchmark/server cycle. It removes duplicate ASP scanning/skeleton construction and reduces repeated VB analysis setup."},{"title":"Use operation-specific semantic cache keys instead of serializing the whole context","files":["crates/asp-analysis/src/core_bridge.rs","packages/core/src/vbscript.ts"],"evidence":"semantic_cache_key serializes the entire context and disables caching when the serialized context exceeds 4096 bytes (core_bridge.rs lines 319-332). The Rust collect path only reads limited context fields: context.symbols via add_implicit_assignment_symbols (lines 3339-3350), typeEnvironment/comTypes through build_type_facts/merge_configured_com_types (lines 4289-4301 and 4535-4559), while diagnostics additionally check includeSummaryUris, externalRefUsages, unusedDiagnostics, and typeChecking (lines 4845-4865 and 4917-4919). TS has similar behavior-specific fields and also disables FromText caching when debugStep exists (vbscript.ts lines 3191-3199).","proposed_change":"Build separate canonical cache-key payloads for symbols and diagnostics from only fields that affect each operation. For symbols, include symbols/typeEnvironment/comTypes and include-awareness fields needed by implicit include handling; exclude locale, unusedDiagnostics, externalRefUsages, debug-only fields, and unrelated documents when they do not affect symbol output. For diagnostics, include only diagnostic-affecting fields. Hash the canonical payload rather than embedding large JSON directly, so large but stable contexts can still cache.","risk":"Medium. The optimization is safe only if every result-affecting context field is included. Add regression tests that change each included and excluded field to prove hits/misses are correct.","expected_perf_effect":"Medium on real LSP workloads with rich project contexts, where current full-context serialization can either miss due to irrelevant field changes or disable caching over the 4096-byte cap."},{"title":"Memoize TypeAnnotations and Option Explicit inside VbAnalysisCache","files":["crates/asp-analysis/src/core_bridge.rs"],"evidence":"apply_type_annotations calls parse_type_annotations once during symbol collection (lines 3398-3400). infer_assigned_types runs two passes (lines 4016-4032), and each pass builds type facts; build_type_facts calls parse_type_annotations(analysis).members again (lines 4289-4327). Strict diagnostics call build_type_facts again (lines 4917-4929), causing another annotation scan. Option Explicit also lowercases the whole analysis text in add_implicit_assignment_symbols (lines 3328-3333) and diagnose_vbscript_with_analysis (lines 4790-4793).","proposed_change":"Add precomputed fields to VbAnalysisCache such as type_annotations: TypeAnnotations and has_option_explicit: bool. Have apply_type_annotations and build_type_facts consume references to the cached annotations, and have implicit-symbol/diagnostic checks read has_option_explicit instead of allocating a lowercased full-document string repeatedly.","risk":"Low. parse_type_annotations and Option Explicit detection are pure over VbAnalysisCache input. The main care point is preserving current case-insensitive matching and comments/offset behavior exactly.","expected_perf_effect":"Small to medium generally; larger for comment-heavy files, strict typeChecking, or repeated type-fact builds during analyzeVbscript."},{"title":"Avoid linear doc-comment token search for every symbol node","files":["crates/asp-analysis/src/core_bridge.rs","packages/core/src/vbscript.ts"],"evidence":"Rust collect_symbols_from_analysis passes each region's document_tokens to collect_symbols_from_node (lines 2826-2838). collect_symbols_from_node asks documentation_for_node for classes, procedures, and declarations (for example lines 2899-2900, 2975-2976, and 3032-3034). documentation_for_node finds the first token at/after the node by scanning document_tokens from the beginning with position(...) for each node (lines 3122-3128), then walks backward through comments. The TS path already passes a createDocCommentLookup(node) object into addSymbolsFromVbNode (vbscript.ts lines 3066-3074), indicating the optimized shape is to keep lookup state rather than restart a token scan per symbol.","proposed_change":"In Rust, replace documentation_for_node's repeated position scan with either binary_search_by on token.start or a per-region cursor/lookup stored in VbRegionAnalysis. Keep the backward comment walk unchanged so documentation content and LSP symbol documentation shape remain identical.","risk":"Low. Token order is already by start offset; preserving the same nearest-preceding-comment rules should keep behavior stable. Add parity tests around adjacent comments, whitespace/newline gaps, XML doc comments, and declarations without docs.","expected_perf_effect":"Medium for files with many declarations because it changes doc lookup from roughly O(symbols * tokens) to O(symbols * log tokens) or amortized O(symbols + tokens)."}],"verification":["No files were edited, staged, committed, or pushed for this read-only packet.","Run `cargo test -p asp-analysis` after implementing Rust bridge changes, with focused assertions for collectVbscriptSymbols/analyzeVbscript result equality before and after cache hits.","Run `pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core run test` to cover TS fallback parity, symbol shapes, diagnostics, UTF-16 ranges, and public API behavior.","Run `pnpm run benchmark:huge` with cold and hot cache modes. Also run `ASP_LSP_BENCH_DEBUG_STEPS=1 pnpm run benchmark:huge` only for step attribution, because debugStep intentionally disables TS FromText caching.","Add a small benchmark or test that calls collectVbscriptSymbolsFromTextAsync and analyzeVbscriptFromTextAsync on the same source twice, verifying the second call avoids duplicate parse/analysis setup and returns byte-for-byte equivalent JSON/LSP shapes."],"blockers":[]}

objective: Design narrow verification for behavior and benchmark targets.
focus: tests, cargo/pnpm checks, benchmark commands, target assertions
result_json: {"packet_id":"P3","findings":[{"title":"Use skeleton/hydration tests plus huge benchmark phases to guard async parse and VB analysis shortcuts","files":["packages/core/test/parser.test.ts","scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs","crates/asp-lsp-server/tests/stdio.rs"],"evidence":"parser.test.ts covers skeleton parse hydration and async summary/symbol helpers at lines 47-84. benchmark-huge.mjs measures parseAspDocument, buildVirtualDocuments, collectVbscriptSymbols, and analyzeVbscript separately at lines 41-64, with optional ASP_LSP_BENCH_DEBUG_STEPS at lines 18 and 84-90. stdio.rs covers fast parser then debounced semantic diagnostics at lines 301-344.","proposed_change":"For parse/VB analysis optimizations, verify the existing skeleton hydration behavior first, then compare benchmark-huge cold vs hot medians for parseAspDocument, collectVbscriptSymbols, analyzeVbscript, and debug step totals. Keep the expected LSP sequence: first empty fast diagnostics, then semantic diagnostics with missingName.","risk":"High if hydration is skipped too aggressively: CST-backed symbols, diagnostics, hovers, or result ordering can change even when raw parse output still looks valid.","expected_perf_effect":"Lower first-pass parse latency and reduced duplicate VBScript CST/analysis work on huge documents; most visible in parseAspDocument and analyzeVbscript median times."},{"title":"Protect incremental edit optimizations with safe/fallback and UTF-16 offset assertions","files":["packages/core/test/parser.test.ts","crates/asp-lsp-server/tests/stdio.rs"],"evidence":"parser.test.ts asserts safe HTML and VBScript incremental updates at lines 86-128, unsafe/full fallback and boundary edits at lines 131-200, and UTF-16 range shifting with an emoji at lines 203-219. stdio.rs asserts didChange clears diagnostics without changing LSP diagnostic shape at lines 99-158.","proposed_change":"Any optimization around change handling should keep these exact tests as the narrow behavior gate, and add only targeted cases if the optimization touches region boundaries or range math. Benchmark impact should be interpreted through reduced parse/analyze work in benchmark-huge rather than by changing public didChange/result shapes.","risk":"Medium: edit coalescing or range math mistakes can corrupt UTF-16 LSP ranges or treat unsafe Classic ASP/IIS delimiter edits as incremental.","expected_perf_effect":"Better latency after small safe edits by avoiding full parse and full analysis; no intended change to LSP responses."},{"title":"Use embedded operation split to verify virtual-document and diagnostic cache changes","files":["scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs","crates/asp-lsp-server/tests/stdio.rs","packages/core/test/parser.test.ts"],"evidence":"embedded-language-benchmark.mjs separates html/css/javascript virtual document and diagnostic operations at lines 35-45 and measures them with sample/cache/concurrency controls at lines 617-645. It exercises caches for virtual docs, diagnostics, TextDocument, CSS stylesheet, JS language service, and JS source file at lines 23-33 and 315-396. stdio.rs covers embedded CSS/TypeScript diagnostics at lines 352-397 and embedded completion/hover/definition/color/linked-editing read requests at lines 2562-2808. parser.test.ts checks virtual document masking/source maps at lines 259-321.","proposed_change":"For embedded-language optimizations, compare ASP_LSP_BENCH_SAMPLE=large/huge/include-tree with ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=cold and hot, and test ASP_LSP_BENCH_CONCURRENCY=1 and 4. Treat javascriptSemanticDiagnostics, javascriptUnusedDiagnostics, and javascriptDiagnostics as separate targets so a JS fast path cannot hide extra work in combined diagnostics.","risk":"High: virtual document caching can easily stale source maps, hide inline ASP islands, or change TypeScript/CSS diagnostic ranges.","expected_perf_effect":"Reduced repeated virtual-document construction, TextDocument/source-map remapping, CSS stylesheet parsing, and TypeScript service/source-file setup for embedded-heavy pages."},{"title":"Guard workspace and sidecar cache optimizations with cache invalidation tests and hot/cold benchmark contrast","files":["crates/asp-lsp-server/tests/stdio.rs","scripts/benchmark-cache-mode.mjs","scripts/benchmark-huge.mjs","scripts/embedded-language-benchmark.mjs"],"evidence":"stdio.rs verifies sidecar project cache invalidation after watched file changes and fingerprint telemetry at lines 401-520, workspace disk cache hits and equality across clears at lines 2335-2485, and background analysis warming at lines 2490-2557. benchmark-cache-mode.mjs makes cold runs synthesize unique URIs and hot runs reuse sources at lines 3-25; both benchmark-huge and embedded benchmark read that mode.","proposed_change":"For cache/fingerprint optimizations, require the stdio cache tests plus before/after hot-vs-cold benchmark runs. The target assertion is that hot medians improve while cold medians do not regress materially, and stale workspace/sidecar diagnostics are still invalidated after watched files, clearProcessCache, clearDiskCache, and clearCache.","risk":"High: cache-key shortcuts can return stale diagnostics or stale embedded project state while benchmarks still look faster.","expected_perf_effect":"Improved hot-path workspace diagnostics, embedded diagnostics, and repeated huge-file analysis without changing cold correctness."}],"verification":["Behavior: `pnpm --filter @asp-lsp/core run test -- test/parser.test.ts` to cover parser, virtual document, incremental edit, UTF-16, formatting, and VBScript analysis assertions.","Behavior: `cargo test -p asp-lsp-server --test stdio` as the broad LSP result-shape gate; for faster triage run individual tests named in the findings, especially `applies_incremental_change_over_stdio_lsp`, `publishes_fast_parser_then_debounced_vbscript_diagnostics`, `publishes_embedded_sidecar_diagnostics_over_stdio_lsp`, `invalidates_embedded_sidecar_project_cache_after_watched_file_change`, `serves_workspace_diagnostics_with_disk_cache`, `warms_workspace_diagnostics_with_background_analysis`, and `serves_embedded_read_requests_over_stdio_lsp`.","Sanity: `pnpm run typecheck` and `pnpm run lint`; add `cargo check -p asp-lsp-server` for Rust-side cache or LSP changes.","Benchmark parse/VB targets: `ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=10 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=2 ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=cold pnpm run benchmark:huge` and the same with `ASP_LSP_BENCH_CACHE_MODE=hot`; compare median/mean for parseAspDocument, buildVirtualDocuments, collectVbscriptSymbols, analyzeVbscript, and embedded operations.","Benchmark analysis-step target: `ASP_LSP_BENCH_DEBUG_STEPS=1 ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=10 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=2 pnpm run benchmark:huge`; assert the optimized step total drops without moving diagnostics/symbol behavior.","Benchmark embedded targets: `ASP_LSP_BENCH_SAMPLE=huge ASP_LSP_BENCH_ITERATIONS=10 ASP_LSP_BENCH_WARMUPS=2 ASP_LSP_BENCH_CONCURRENCY=1 pnpm run benchmark:embedded` and repeat with `ASP_LSP_BENCH_CONCURRENCY=4`, plus cold/hot cache modes; compare per-operation medians for html/css/javascript virtual documents and diagnostics."],"blockers":["Read-only packet: I did not run pnpm/cargo tests or benchmarks because they can write build artifacts, target outputs, dist files, generated samples, or caches.","Current benchmark scripts print human-readable tables only; there is no machine-readable baseline/threshold assertion in the focused files, so performance pass/fail still needs manual median/mean comparison or a separate harness.","benchmark-huge.mjs always invokes the huge sample generator before measuring, and embedded-language-benchmark.mjs also invokes sample generators in main; verify generated-sample churn before using these in a strict read-only or clean-tree workflow."]}
